The '''R-41 Starchaser''' was a multi-role [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] manufactured by [[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]]

==Characteristics==
The Starchaser was built as a solid starfighter capable of competing with the [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]].<ref name="Target" /> It had a good balance of speed, weapons and durability, but lacked heavy armor or internal hull bracing, which often made it vulnerable to well-placed enemy hits. It had a slight advantage over the Headhunter with its [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and in the hands of expert [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] could pose a considerable threat even to newer starfighters, as was the case in the clash between the [[Rneekii]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] at [[Kiilimaar]].

The craft featured a flat lifting body with a diamond-shaped nose. It had [[S-foils/Legends|S-foils]] mounted aft, and was powered by four H-K [[Model 4Yb ion fusion engine]]s as well as a Class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]].<ref name="Target" />

The ship's drawbacks, which included light armor, low maneuverability, and low-powered shields prevented its success on the market.<ref name="Target" />

A two-seat version, named the [[R-42 Starchaser]], existed; however, this version, despite being superior in some aspects, proved less popular with the primary market of the R-41 and only a few hundred were ever built.<ref name="TFUCG" />

==History==
[[File:R-41_Attack.jpg|thumb|left|150px|An R-41 Starchaser in action.]]
Production delays meant the craft came out later than the Headhunters.<ref name="Target" />

By the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], this starfighter was already outmatched by newer starships and by the time of the conflict between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], it was generally considered outdated. This did not prevent the resource-strapped Rebellion from using the R-41 in early battles against the Empire's newer [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Fighters]], as evidenced in the [[Assault on Kamino]]. The R-41 seemed to disappear from the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel fleet]] once the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] became available. Cash-strapped Rebel cells were forced to use it, though.<ref name="Target" />

Later in the Galactic Civil War it was still found in the hands of third party organizations, such as [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] like the [[Rneekii]], who preferred it because of its reliability, since regular maintenance was not often available for these groups.

The R-41 was the favorite ship of [[Abron Mar]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Takara]]. [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] stole an R-41 while escaping from [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Kaerobani]]'s base on [[Rathalay]] with a recovered [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].
